title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft
label: mx1_transmute_04
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
The problem is a combinatorial optimization task involving bitstring prefix/substring ambiguity. It requires finding a minimal extension to a set of binary strings such that no string becomes a prefix of another under specific truncation rules.

final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: Undefined
JUSTIFICATION: No specific input strings provided to calculate minimum length.

assessment.summary: This is the first scored attempt in the loop history.

what_helped: The loop preserved the question and family boundary so changes stay interpretable.
The current prompt is isolated as the only variable being optimized.
what_hurt: The run still has room to improve in either correctness or efficiency.

recommendation.reason: The answers are still wrong across multiple attempts, so the next prompt should pivot hard to a different reasoning strategy.
recommendation.new_prompt_text: Rewrite the problem so the next agent first checks whether the smallest candidate answer values are even possible, using modular arithmetic or direct contradiction before searching more broadly.

history_observations: the loop has plateaued enough to justify a new direction
